skill.name: canva-automation
skill.description: Automates Canva workflows through Rube MCP for browsing designs, creating designs, uploading assets, exporting files, managing folders, and using brand templates.
skill.summary: Control common Canva design operations through Rube MCP and Canva OAuth.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows the command and weak-crypto alerts are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and ordinary Canva terms such as design. No executable code, prompt injection, shell execution, or cryptographic implementation was found in SKILL.md. The remaining meaningful risk is that the skill directs users to a third-party Rube MCP endpoint and Canva OAuth workflows that can access Canva designs, assets, exports, and folders.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP and Canva OAuth Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and connect Canva through OAuth. This is a legitimate integration path, but it means a third-party MCP service can broker requests involving Canva designs, uploads, exports, folders, and brand templates. Users should only enable it when they trust the MCP provider and understand the Canva permissions granted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly requires a remote MCP endpoint and Canva OAuth connection, and later workflows operate on user designs and assets. This is a clear data-access risk, but it appears aligned with the stated purpose rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are inline Markdown references to MCP tool names, parameters, and workflow tokens. I did not find shell syntax, scripts, subprocess calls, or instructions to execute local commands in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown text and code-formatted tool names, not executable Ruby or shell code. No evidence found of command execution semantics in the file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text-Matching False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary words and Canva workflow terms, especially repeated references to designs. SKILL.md contains no cryptographic algorithm use, hashing API, encryption code, or security-sensitive crypto guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a Canva workflow guide and the cited lines discuss designs, exports, or tool names. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Workflow Status Checks
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts map to Canva job status, file size, folder, and identifier guidance. These checks are required for asynchronous Canva API workflows and do not inspect the host system or scan networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is Canva API polling and object lookup, not local system discovery or network scanning. No evidence found of host enumeration, port scanning, or credential discovery.

content.value_statement: Canva teams often repeat export, upload, folder, and template tasks by hand. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow guide for using Canva through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Canva OAuth connection checks.
Lists Canva designs with search, ownership filters, sorting, and pagination guidance.
Creates Canva designs from design types, dimensions, templates, or uploaded assets.
Uploads assets and polls asynchronous upload jobs until completion.
Exports Canva designs to formats such as PDF, PNG, JPG, SVG, MP4, GIF, and PPTX.
Organizes designs with folders and starts autofill jobs from brand templates.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP availability and an active Canva connection before workflows can run.
Depends on current MCP tool schemas, so the assistant must search tools before use.
Cannot bypass Canva permissions, rate limits, file size limits, or format restrictions.
Export, upload, and autofill results are asynchronous and require polling.

use_cases.title: Prepare Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Find existing campaign designs, upload approved images, export final files, and keep assets organized in Canva folders.

use_cases.title: Generate Template-Based Designs
use_cases.description: Use brand templates with exact placeholder data to create consistent designs for launches, events, or sales teams.

use_cases.title: Manage Design Libraries
use_cases.description: Browse large Canva libraries, resolve design IDs, move items into folders, and handle paginated results predictably.

prompt_templates.title: List My Designs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Canva automation skill to search my Canva designs for [topic]. Show matching titles, owners, modified dates, and any pagination needed.

prompt_templates.title: Export a Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Canva design named [design name], export pages [pages] as [format], poll until complete, and give me the download link and status.

prompt_templates.title: Create and Organize Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload this public asset URL to Canva as [asset name], wait for upload success, create folder [folder name], and move the related design into it.

prompt_templates.title: Autofill Brand Templates
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the brand template named [template name], confirm required placeholders, start an autofill job with my campaign data, and report the generated design ID.

output_examples.input: Export the Q3 webinar deck as a high-quality PDF.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the deck, starts a PDF export job, polls until it succeeds, and returns the temporary download link with completion status.

output_examples.input: Create social posts from our product launch brand template.
output_examples.output: The assistant locates the brand template, maps provided campaign values to placeholders, starts autofill, and reports the new design name and ID.

output_examples.input: Organize last month's event designs into a folder.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists matching designs, creates or selects the target folder, moves each design, and reports which items were updated.

best_practices: Search Rube tools before each workflow so parameter names match the current schema.
Confirm the Canva connection is active before reading, exporting, uploading, or moving content.
Poll asynchronous jobs with a short delay and handle failed statuses clearly.
anti_patterns: Do not assume design names are unique; resolve the correct design ID before changing or exporting content.
Do not upload private files through public URLs unless the user accepts that exposure path.
Do not reuse expired export download URLs; create a fresh export when needed.

faq.question: Does this skill run local scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide for using Rube MCP tools with Canva. It does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: What connection is required?
faq.answer: Rube MCP must be configured, and Canva must be connected through the returned OAuth flow.

faq.question: Can it create new Canva designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes how to create designs with Canva design types, optional assets, and custom dimensions when supported.

faq.question: Can it export existing designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides export jobs for formats such as PDF, PNG, JPG, SVG, MP4, GIF, and PPTX.

faq.question: Why does it search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameter names and requirements.

faq.question: What data should users review before enabling it?
faq.answer: Users should review the Canva OAuth permissions and decide whether they trust the Rube MCP provider with design workflow access.
